assistant:

```
POST /iam/api-keys
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "name": <string>,
  "key_hash": <string>,
  "user_id": <uuid.UUID>,
  "organization_id": <uuid.UUID>,
  "scopes": <pq.StringArray>,
  "is_active": <bool>,
  "last_used_at": <time.Time>,
  "expires_at": <time.Time>,

  "updated_at": <time.Time>,
}
```

Returns the created `ApiKeys` object with HTTP 201 on success.

| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["price","between",[100,500]]]` |

user: What filter operators are available for `Permissions` API queries?
assistant:
Complete filter operator reference for `Permissions` API.

Filters use a **JSON array-of-rules** format: `filters=[[column, operator, value], ...]`

| Operator | Description | Example value |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `eq` | Equals | `[["name","eq","active"]]` |
| `neq` | Not equals | `[["name","neq","deleted"]]` |
| `gt` | Greater than | `[["price","gt",100]]` |
| `gte` | Greater or equal | `[["price","gte",100]]` |
| `lt` | Less than | `[["price","lt",500]]` |
| `lte` | Less or equal | `[["price","lte",500]]` |
| `in` | In array | `[["name","in",["a","b"]]]` |
| `notin` | Not in array | `[["name","notin",["x","y"]]]` |
| `like` | Pattern match (case-sensitive) | `[["name","like","test%"]]` |
| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["price","between",[100,500]]]` |

Multiple filters are ANDed together:
```
GET /iam/permissions/pagination?filters=[["status","eq","active"],["price","gte",100]]
```

user: What filter operators are available for `Sessions` API queries?

Filters use a **JSON array-of-rules** format: `filters=[[column, operator, value], ...]`

| Operator | Description | Example value |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `eq` | Equals | `[["name","eq","active"]]` |
| `neq` | Not equals | `[["name","neq","deleted"]]` |
| `gt` | Greater than | `[["price","gt",100]]` |
| `gte` | Greater or equal | `[["price","gte",100]]` |
| `lt` | Less than | `[["price","lt",500]]` |
| `lte` | Less or equal | `[["price","lte",500]]` |
| `in` | In array | `[["name","in",["a","b"]]]` |
| `notin` | Not in array | `[["name","notin",["x","y"]]]` |
| `like` | Pattern match (case-sensitive) | `[["name","like","test%"]]` |
| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["price","between",[100,500]]]` |

Multiple filters are ANDed together:

| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["price","between",[100,500]]]` |

user: What filter operators are available for `Categories` API queries?

| `like` | Pattern match (case-sensitive) | `[["name","like","test%"]]` |
| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["sort_order","between",[100,500]]]` |

user: What filter operators are available for `PriceHistory` API queries?

Complete filter operator reference for `PriceHistory` API.

Filters use a **JSON array-of-rules** format: `filters=[[column, operator, value], ...]`

| Operator | Description | Example value |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `eq` | Equals | `[["name","eq","active"]]` |
| `neq` | Not equals | `[["name","neq","deleted"]]` |
| `gt` | Greater than | `[["old_price","gt",100]]` |
| `gte` | Greater or equal | `[["old_price","gte",100]]` |
| `lt` | Less than | `[["old_price","lt",500]]` |

| `in` | In array | `[["name","in",["a","b"]]]` |
| `notin` | Not in array | `[["name","notin",["x","y"]]]` |
| `like` | Pattern match (case-sensitive) | `[["name","like","test%"]]` |
| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["old_price","between",[100,500]]]` |

Multiple filters are ANDed together:
```
GET /catalog/price-history/pagination?filters=[["status","eq","active"],["old_price","gte",100]]

assistant:
Use **groupby** and **aggregations** for aggregate queries on `Products`:

**GroupBy** â€” groups results by a field (returned as `map[groupKey][]T` or paginated groups):
```
GET /catalog/products/pagination?groupby=name
```

**Aggregations** â€” compute aggregate functions; combines with `groupby`:
```
GET /catalog/products/pagination?groupby=name&aggregations=[{"func":"count","field":"name","alias":"total"},{"func":"sum","field":"base_price","alias":"total_base_price"}]

Supported aggregation functions: `count`, `sum`, `avg`, `min`, `max`.

user: What filter operators are available for `Products` API queries?
assistant:
Complete filter operator reference for `Products` API.

Filters use a **JSON array-of-rules** format: `filters=[[column, operator, value], ...]`

| Operator | Description | Example value |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `eq` | Equals | `[["name","eq","active"]]` |
| `neq` | Not equals | `[["name","neq","deleted"]]` |
| `gt` | Greater than | `[["base_price","gt",100]]` |
| `gte` | Greater or equal | `[["base_price","gte",100]]` |
| `lt` | Less than | `[["base_price","lt",500]]` |
| `lte` | Less or equal | `[["base_price","lte",500]]` |
| `in` | In array | `[["name","in",["a","b"]]]` |
| `notin` | Not in array | `[["name","notin",["x","y"]]]` |
| `like` | Pattern match (case-sensitive) | `[["name","like","test%"]]` |
| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_featured","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_featured","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["base_price","between",[100,500]]]` |

Multiple filters are ANDed together:

user: What filter operators are available for `Customers` API queries?
assistant:
Complete filter operator reference for `Customers` API.

Filters use a **JSON array-of-rules** format: `filters=[[column, operator, value], ...]`

| Operator | Description | Example value |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `eq` | Equals | `[["name","eq","active"]]` |
| `neq` | Not equals | `[["name","neq","deleted"]]` |
| `gt` | Greater than | `[["loyalty_points","gt",100]]` |
| `gte` | Greater or equal | `[["loyalty_points","gte",100]]` |
| `lt` | Less than | `[["loyalty_points","lt",500]]` |
| `lte` | Less or equal | `[["loyalty_points","lte",500]]` |
| `in` | In array | `[["name","in",["a","b"]]]` |
| `notin` | Not in array | `[["name","notin",["x","y"]]]` |
| `like` | Pattern match (case-sensitive) | `[["name","like","test%"]]` |
| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["loyalty_points","between",[100,500]]]` |

Multiple filters are ANDed together:
```

user: What filter operators are available for `Orders` API queries?
assistant:
Complete filter operator reference for `Orders` API.

Filters use a **JSON array-of-rules** format: `filters=[[column, operator, value], ...]`

| Operator | Description | Example value |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `eq` | Equals | `[["name","eq","active"]]` |
| `neq` | Not equals | `[["name","neq","deleted"]]` |
| `gt` | Greater than | `[["subtotal","gt",100]]` |
| `gte` | Greater or equal | `[["subtotal","gte",100]]` |
| `lt` | Less than | `[["subtotal","lt",500]]` |
| `lte` | Less or equal | `[["subtotal","lte",500]]` |
| `in` | In array | `[["name","in",["a","b"]]]` |
| `notin` | Not in array | `[["name","notin",["x","y"]]]` |
| `like` | Pattern match (case-sensitive) | `[["name","like","test%"]]` |
| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["subtotal","between",[100,500]]]` |

Multiple filters are ANDed together:
```
GET /orders/orders/pagination?filters=[["status","eq","active"],["subtotal","gte",100]]
```

user: What filter operators are available for `Payments` API queries?
assistant:

Filters use a **JSON array-of-rules** format: `filters=[[column, operator, value], ...]`

| Operator | Description | Example value |

| `gt` | Greater than | `[["amount","gt",100]]` |
| `gte` | Greater or equal | `[["amount","gte",100]]` |
| `lt` | Less than | `[["amount","lt",500]]` |
| `lte` | Less or equal | `[["amount","lte",500]]` |
| `in` | In array | `[["name","in",["a","b"]]]` |
| `notin` | Not in array | `[["name","notin",["x","y"]]]` |
| `like` | Pattern match (case-sensitive) | `[["name","like","test%"]]` |
| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["amount","between",[100,500]]]` |

Multiple filters are ANDed together:
```
GET /orders/payments/pagination?filters=[["status","eq","active"],["amount","gte",100]]

| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["price","between",[100,500]]]` |

| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |

| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["resolved","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["resolved","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["price","between",[100,500]]]` |

user: What filter operators are available for `Metrics` API queries?
assistant:
Complete filter operator reference for `Metrics` API.

Filters use a **JSON array-of-rules** format: `filters=[[column, operator, value], ...]`

| Operator | Description | Example value |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `eq` | Equals | `[["name","eq","active"]]` |
| `neq` | Not equals | `[["name","neq","deleted"]]` |
| `gt` | Greater than | `[["value","gt",100]]` |
| `gte` | Greater or equal | `[["value","gte",100]]` |
| `lt` | Less than | `[["value","lt",500]]` |
| `lte` | Less or equal | `[["value","lte",500]]` |
| `in` | In array | `[["name","in",["a","b"]]]` |
| `notin` | Not in array | `[["name","notin",["x","y"]]]` |
| `like` | Pattern match (case-sensitive) | `[["name","like","test%"]]` |
| `ilike` | Pattern match (case-insensitive) | `[["name","ilike","%test%"]]` |
| `is` | Is null / is true | `[["is_active","is",null]]` |
| `isnot` | Is not null / is not true | `[["is_active","isnot",null]]` |
| `between` | Between two values (inclusive) | `[["value","between",[100,500]]]` |

Multiple filters are ANDed together:
```
GET /analytics/metrics/pagination?filters=[["status","eq","active"],["value","gte",100]]
```
